methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $7.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Ouster, a publicly traded semiconductor company founded by Angus Pacala, is evaluated against a peer universe of 12 same-sector companies spanning both public and private stages. The comparable set includes five named peers—PsiQuantum ($7.0B, Series E), Etched ($5.0B, Series A), SambaNova Systems ($5.0B, Series D), Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B, public), and Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D)—alongside seven additional private firms across early to late stages.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. Across the 12-company cohort in Chips & Semiconductors, the median valuation stands at $2.2B, with a range spanning $500M to $7.0B. Public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trade at a sector P/S median of 24.4x, but cannot be applied directly given the absence of Ouster's disclosed revenue. Accordingly, peer-median anchoring provides the sole quantitative basis for valuation inference.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.8B to $2.5B, centered on $2.2B. Ouster's current $1.5B valuation sits below this implied median by approximately $700M. The primary source of uncertainty is the wide dispersion within the peer set ($500M to $7.0B), which reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity, market timing, and funding stage despite sector alignment. Revenue-based cross-checks and margin profiles would refine this range materially.
